theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: The Sumerian Lexicon of Gesture: Extended Analysis Primary Registry IDs: 0001–0006 (TZP geometry — the seven sign vocabulary maps to seven structural levels) Secondary IDs: 0060–0085 (rotational quantisation and toroidal structure — ŠU as operator) Tertiary IDs: 852–853 (cymatic stability — SILIM as attractor state) The extended lexicon identifies seven gesture signs forming a semantic arc from AŠ (singularity/fist) to SILIM (wholeness/two open palms). This arc maps directly onto the seven primary structural levels of the TZP registry:
theory.technical_interpretation: e scribe's hand applying stylus to clay was not merely a physical act; it was a ritual application of the ŠU principle — the beginning of work, the moment at which intention becomes inscription. **The Trawin Hamiltonian is the mathematical ŠU: the operator that, when applied, begins the dynamics of the TZP system.** --- ## SILIM and the Lyapunov Attractor SILIM — health, peace, wholeness, greeting — is the state of a system at its stable attractor. The Lyapunov stability condition (ID 853) guarantees that the stochastic cymatic field converges on its target state $\Phi_\text{target}$: When the system reaches $\Phi_\text{target}$, the distance $V[\Phi]$ reaches zero. Nothing is missing. Nothing is broken. The system is in its state of maximum wholeness. This is SILIM. The Lyapunov attractor is the mathematical expression of health and peace in a dynamical system: the state toward which al In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

dictionary_sections.definition: The Sumerian Lexicon Extended is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation V[\Phi] = \|\Phi - \Phi_\text{target}\|^2, \quad \frac{dV}{dt} < 0. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.

This arc maps directly onto the seven primary structural levels of the TZP registry: The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records V[\Phi] = \|\Phi - \Phi_\text{target}\|^2, \quad \frac{dV}{dt} < 0; \lim_{\epsilon\to 0}\text{Vol}(B_\epsilon\cap\Sigma)/\text{Vol}(B_\epsilon) = 2; \hat{H}_\text{Trawin}; \hat{H}_\text{Trawin}|\psi\rangle. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.

dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: The Sumerian Lexicon Extended extends the registry beyond the first thousand IDs by giving the new band a cited mathematical object, a text-rich dictionary page, and a derivation path attached to the exact source file.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
